beaches.name: Bora Bora, French Polynesia
beaches.description: A jewel of the South Pacific, Bora Bora captivates with its turquoise lagoon, dramatic volcanic peaks, and legendary overwater bungalows. Ideal for snorkeling, diving, and ultimate relaxation.

beaches.name: Maldives, Indian Ocean
beaches.description: A string of 1,200 coral islands boasting crystal-clear waters, vibrant marine life, and pristine white-sand beaches. The Maldives is the ultimate tropical escape.

beaches.name: Whitehaven Beach, Australia
beaches.description: Stretching 7 km through the Whitsundays, Whitehaven Beach is famed for its pure silica sand and swirling turquoise tidal patterns. A true natural wonder.

beaches.name: Navagio Beach, Greece
beaches.description: Enclosed by towering white cliffs on the island of Zakynthos, Navagio (Shipwreck Beach) is one of the most photographed beaches in the world.

temples.name: Angkor Wat, Cambodia
temples.description: The largest religious monument in the world, Angkor Wat is a breathtaking 12th-century masterpiece of Khmer architecture, surrounded by a massive moat and intricate bas-reliefs.

temples.name: Senso-ji Temple, Japan
temples.description: Tokyo's oldest and most iconic temple, Senso-ji in Asakusa draws millions of visitors yearly. Its magnificent Thunder Gate and five-story pagoda are legendary landmarks.

temples.name: Wat Phra Kaew, Thailand
temples.description: Home to the sacred Emerald Buddha, Wat Phra Kaew within Bangkok's Grand Palace complex is Thailand's most revered temple, glittering with gold and intricate mosaics.

temples.name: Tikal Temple, Guatemala
temples.description: Rising above the jungle canopy in northern Guatemala, the ancient Maya temples of Tikal are among the most impressive pre-Columbian ruins in the Americas.

countries.name: Japan
countries.description: A country of stunning contrasts — from neon-lit Tokyo streets and ancient Kyoto temples to serene Mount Fuji and cherry blossom-lined parks. Japan is an unmissable destination.

cities.name: Tokyo
cities.description: Japan's sprawling capital blends ultra-modern technology with deep-rooted traditions. Explore Shibuya's famous crossing, Shinjuku's vibrant nightlife, and serene Meiji Shrine.

cities.name: Kyoto
cities.description: Once Japan's imperial capital, Kyoto is a living museum of geisha culture, bamboo forests, 2,000 temples and shrines, and exquisite traditional cuisine.

countries.name: Australia
countries.description: From the iconic Sydney Opera House and Great Barrier Reef to the red deserts of the Outback, Australia offers extraordinary diversity across its vast landscapes.

cities.name: Sydney
cities.description: A harbour city of world-famous beauty, Sydney enchants with its iconic Opera House, Harbour Bridge, Bondi Beach, and a vibrant cosmopolitan food and arts scene.

cities.name: Melbourne
cities.description: Australia's cultural capital, Melbourne is celebrated for its world-class coffee, eclectic laneways, thriving arts scene, and passionate sporting culture.

countries.name: Canada
countries.description: Canada's incredible diversity spans the multicultural vibrancy of Toronto and Vancouver, the French-Canadian charm of Montréal, and the awe-inspiring Rockies and Niagara Falls.

cities.name: Toronto
cities.description: Canada's largest city pulses with world-class dining, the CN Tower, vibrant neighbourhoods from Kensington Market to Distillery District, and a thriving multicultural energy.

cities.name: Vancouver
cities.description: Nestled between mountains and ocean, Vancouver is consistently ranked among the world's most livable cities, with stunning natural scenery and a buzzing Pacific Rim food culture.
